column1: #mapping_set_description: LLM-generated semantic alignment between the FIX Protocol LinkML schema (structural metamodel of FIX Latest chapters) and the FIX Orchestra LinkML schema (the FIX Trading Community machine-readable companion). Covers metamodel-to-metamodel correspondences for messages, components, fields, datatypes, categories, presence, layers, and scenarios.

column8: comment
Allocation communication scenarios correspond to Orchestra workflow ScenarioType.
Both classify message/field groupings; FIX Latest BusinessArea is a top-level grouping equivalent to an Orchestra CategoryType.
Permissible values name the top-level FIX categories represented by Orchestra CategoryType instances.
CommonComponent is a business-area-scoped instance of the Orchestra ComponentType metamodel.
Both describe a named reusable group of FIX fields at the metamodel level.
An Extension Pack is a versioned unit of change applied to a Repository; loose relation.
Both are the metamodel description of a FIX Protocol datatype.
Permissible values enumerate the names of Orchestra Datatype instances.
FIX Family Standard names overlap with Orchestra ProtocolEnum permissible values (FIX4, FIXT, FIXP, FIXML, FAST, SBE, ...).
Both serve as a top-level container root for the FIX specification; Orchestra Repository is broader.
Both define a FIX field with tag number, name, and datatype.
Required-status enum aligns with Orchestra Presence (required, optional, forbidden, ignored, constant).
GlobalComponent is a cross-area-scoped instance of the Orchestra ComponentType metamodel.
Infrastructure-specific category names are narrower than the generic Orchestra CategoryType.
Both define a FIX application message at the metamodel level.
Both express a category grouping of messages.
Permissible values name the message categories represented by CategoryType instances.
Post-Trade-specific category names are narrower than the generic Orchestra CategoryType.
Per-category Post-Trade message description block; instance-of MessageType in Orchestra terms.
Pre-Trade-specific category names are narrower than the generic Orchestra CategoryType.
FIX product/asset-class coverage loosely aligns with Orchestra CategoryType classification.
FIX session-layer protocol names align with the Orchestra Layer.session permissible value.
SpecificComponent is a category-scoped instance of the Orchestra ComponentType metamodel.
Layer of the FIX Technical Standard Stack corresponds directly to Orchestra Layer.
Documented usages of TradeCaptureReport(35=AE) correspond to Orchestra ScenarioType.
Trade-specific category names are narrower than the generic Orchestra CategoryType.
